            # Blueprint Writer Directive — rewrite `sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`

`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`

## Scope (ONLY this section)
Section `sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality` (currently ~L2564–L2786), comprising:

## Why this rewrite (the route pivoted)
The current proof sketches for `lem:pullback_tensor_iso` and `lem:pullback_unit_iso`
describe a **route that cannot be formalized** and must be replaced. The iter-239
prover and two iter-240 audits (lean-vs-blueprint-checker-tensorobjsubstrate;
mathlib-analogist `pullback-monoidal`, persistent file `analogies/pullback-monoidal.md`)
established, with live LSP verification:

1. **`(Presheaf|Sheaf)OfModules.pullback` is an ABSTRACT left adjoint**
   (`(pushforward _).leftAdjoint`) with **no sectionwise and no stalkwise value
   formula** in Mathlib at the pinned commit. So the current Step 2 ("the presheaf
   pullback is, sectionwise, extension of scalars; assemble the sectionwise
   `extendScalars` tensorators") **cannot typecheck** — there is no sectionwise
   `(pullback φ).obj` to attach an `extendScalars` tensorator to.
2. **`Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` does NOT exist.** Mathlib has only
   `Adjunction.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal` (the opposite direction). So the oplax
   comparison maps on the left-adjoint pullback are **not free**; an
   `OplaxMonoidal` structure on `pullback` must be constructed.
3. There is **no Mathlib pullback tensorator** (`pullbackObjTensorToTensor` does
   not exist) and **no `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules R)`** (the abstract
   `Sheaf.monoidalCategory` is only for a fixed value category, not modules over a
   varying ring).

The **Stacks statements remain the correct targets** — keep the `% SOURCE` /
`% SOURCE QUOTE` / `\textit{Source: …}` blocks verbatim for all three lemmas
(`lemma-tensor-product-pullback`, `lemma-pullback-invertible`). Only the PROOF
METHOD prose changes.

## The new route to write — Route Z (local-chart finality), TWO phases

Rewrite the section intro and the two sub-lemma proofs to describe **Route Z**.
The mathematical content (read `analogies/pullback-monoidal.md` for the exact
Mathlib citations the writer should weave in as `\texttt{…}` names):

**Phase 1 — `lem:pullback_unit_iso` (`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`), the cheap half.**
This is reachable directly. The canonical Mathlib map
`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` (the `f^*𝒪_X → 𝒪_Y` comparison) is an
isomorphism whenever the comparison functor is `Final` — Mathlib instance
`SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal`. This is exactly the `i7`
step already used inside the project's axiom-clean `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`
(`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundlePullback.lean:156-193`). For a GENERAL `f`,
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is checked to be an iso by reduction to local charts:
on an affine chart `V ⊆ Y`, factor `V.ι ≫ f` through `g = f.resLE U V` (whose
`Opens.map g.base` is `Final` via `final_of_representablyFlat`), so the restricted
comparison matches `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g` which IS an iso by the Final instance;
globalize "iso on every chart ⇒ global iso" via the project's axiom-clean
`isIso_of_isIso_restrict` (`Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:567`). The naturality
glue (`pullbackComp`, `pullbackId`, `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, `pullbackCongr`)
is the same chain already wired in `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`. State this as the
proof; note the small remaining work is the naturality lemma tying the
restricted global `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` to the local one.

**Phase 2 — `lem:pullback_tensor_iso` (`f^*(M⊗N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`), the real gap.**
There is no Mathlib tensorator on `pullback`, so the comparison map must be built.
Describe the route: (a) construct a `pullbackObjTensorToTensor` comparison
`f^*(A⊗B) → f^*A ⊗ f^*B` (the analogue of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`); (b) prove it
is an isomorphism by the SAME finality chart-chase as Phase 1 — on a Final local
chart the pullback is locally extension-of-scalars, which IS strong monoidal in
`ModuleCat`, so the local comparison is the `extendScalars` tensorator (an iso),
and `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` globalizes; (c) package strong monoidality via the
Mathlib oplax⇒strong wrapper `Functor.CoreMonoidal.ofOplaxMonoidal` (which takes
`[IsIso η]` and `[∀ X Y, IsIso (δ X Y)]` and yields `Monoidal`, with `μIso`/`εIso`
delivering the comparison isos). Note that producing the `OplaxMonoidal` structure
on `pullback` is itself non-free (no `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`) — it is built as
the mate of `pushforward`'s lax structure, or directly from the presheaf
pullback's concrete extension-of-scalars description on charts. Record that the
already-landed private brick `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`
(`Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean ~L884`, "sheafification is monoidal up to the
unit") is the RHS reconciliation the eventual `pullbackTensorIso` consumes after
moving the pullback inside sheafification via `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`.

**`lem:isinvertible_pullback` (the Stacks composite) — KEEP essentially as is.**
Its proof composes `pullbackTensorIso⁻¹ ≫ f^*e ≫ pullbackUnitIso` on the existing
witness — that is correct and matches the verbatim Stacks proof quote already in
the file. Leave the statement, `\uses`, SOURCE blocks, and the composite proof
intact. (Only adjust surrounding prose if it references the dead "extendScalars
sectionwise" framing.)

2564	\section{Pullback-monoidality: invertibility under a general scheme morphism}
2565	\label{sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality}
2566	
2567	The relative Picard consumer (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}) re-bases its
2568	line-bundle carrier onto the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
2569	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}, and its
2570	functorial structure maps are \emph{pullback} maps of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules: the
2571	projection pullback \(\pi_T^*\) along \(C \times_S T \to T\) and the base-change
2572	pullback along \(C \times_S T' \to C \times_S T\). For these maps to descend to the
2573	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) carrier one needs that \emph{pullback preserves
2574	\(\otimes\)-invertibility} for \emph{general} morphisms of schemes --- the
2575	projection and the base-change morphism are neither open immersions nor flat.
2576	
2577	This is a genuinely different fact from the open-immersion compatibility
2578	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, and not a corollary of it. That lemma handles
2579	restriction along an open immersion, and it does so by routing through the
2580	\emph{lax right} adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}/\mathtt{restrictScalars}\), which is
2581	strong monoidal there \emph{only} because an open immersion's structure-sheaf ring
2582	map is a local \emph{isomorphism} (\(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)). A general scheme
2583	morphism has no such isomorphism, so the right-adjoint route is unavailable. The
2584	general case instead uses the \emph{left} adjoint --- pullback \(=\) inverse image
2585	\(=\) extension of scalars --- which is strong monoidal for \emph{any} ring map,
2586	with no iso hypothesis: extension of scalars \(- \otimes_R S\) along \(R \to S\)
2587	carries the tensorator \((M \otimes_R N) \otimes_R S \cong (M \otimes_R S)
2588	\otimes_S (N \otimes_R S)\) unconditionally. This is the whole reason the general
2589	lemma is a separate result.
2590	
2591	\begin{lemma}
2592	[Pullback commutes with $\otimes$ for a general scheme morphism]
2593	  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_iso}
2594	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorIso}
2595	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2596	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let
2597	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
2598	  Writing \(f^* = \mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f\) for the inverse-image functor
2599	  of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules, the canonical comparison
2600	  \[
2601	    f^*(M \otimes_X N)
2602	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2603	    (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N)
2604	  \]
2605	  is an isomorphism, natural in \(M\) and \(N\). No local-freeness, flatness, or
2606	  open-immersion hypothesis is required: it holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
2607	  and all morphisms \(f\).
2608	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
2609	  % \ref{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} (pullback of $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a
2610	  % morphism of ringed spaces commutes with tensor product, functorially)
2611	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L2392-2400).
2612	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2613	  % "\begin{lemma}
2614	  % \label{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}
2615	  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be
2616	  % a morphism of ringed spaces. Let $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$
2617	  % be $\mathcal{O}_Y$-modules. Then
2618	  % $f^*(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_Y} \mathcal{G})
2619	  % = f^*\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} f^*\mathcal{G}$
2620	  % functorially in $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$.
2621	  % \end{lemma}"
2622	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
2623	  \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}: for a morphism of ringed spaces
2624	  \(f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)\) and \(\mathcal{O}_Y\)-modules
2625	  \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\), one has \(f^*(\mathcal{F} \otimes \mathcal{G}) =
2626	  f^*\mathcal{F} \otimes f^*\mathcal{G}\) functorially.}
2627	\end{lemma}
2628	
2629	\begin{proof}
2630	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2631	  The proof mirrors the structure of \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} but routes
2632	  through the \emph{left} adjoint, where no isomorphism hypothesis on the ring map
2633	  is needed.
2634	
2635	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce to the presheaf level).} By
2636	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}, the substrate tensor \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
2637	  sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
2638	  \(M.\mathrm{val} \otimes^{p} N.\mathrm{val}\), where
2639	  \(\otimes^{p} = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\). The pullback
2640	  commutes with sheafification: the genuine Mathlib lemma
2641	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\) --- which is general
2642	  (it holds for any morphism of ring-sheaves, and is already consumed in the
2643	  open-immersion case \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) --- supplies a natural
2644	  isomorphism moving \(f^*\) inside the sheafification. The claim therefore reduces
2645	  to the presheaf-level comparison
2646	  \[
2647	    (\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,(A \otimes^{p} B)
2648	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2649	    \bigl((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A\bigr)
2650	      \;\otimes^{p}\;
2651	    \bigl((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B\bigr),
2652	  \]
2653	  where \(\varphi\) is the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of rings
2654	  induced by \(f\), and \(A = M.\mathrm{val}\), \(B = N.\mathrm{val}\).
2655	
2656	  \emph{Step 2 (the presheaf pullback is strong monoidal).} The presheaf-level
2657	  pullback \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\) is, sectionwise,
2658	  \emph{extension of scalars} along the section ring maps of \(\varphi\) --- the
2659	  left adjoint of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\). Mathlib equips extension of scalars
2660	  along \emph{any} ring homomorphism \(\psi\) with a strong-monoidal structure,
2661	  \((\mathtt{extendScalars}\,\psi).\mathtt{Monoidal}\)
2662	  (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Adjunction.lean}); its
2663	  tensorator is the canonical isomorphism
2664	  \((M \otimes_R N) \otimes_R S \cong (M \otimes_R S) \otimes_S (N \otimes_R S)\),
2665	  valid with \emph{no} hypothesis on \(\psi\). Assembling these sectionwise
2666	  tensorators against the presheaf restriction morphisms furnishes a strong-monoidal
2667	  structure on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), whose monoidal
2668	  comparison \(\mu\) is precisely the presheaf-level isomorphism required by Step~1.
2669	  Sheafifying \(\mu\) and transporting along the
2670	  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) isomorphism of Step~1 yields the asserted
2671	  isomorphism \(f^*(M \otimes_X N) \cong (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N)\). Naturality in
2672	  \(M, N\) is inherited from the naturality of \(\mu\) and of sheafification.
2673	
2674	  The contrast with \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is exactly the adjoint used:
2675	  there the comparison is produced from the \emph{right} adjoint
2676	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}/\mathtt{restrictScalars}\), which is strong monoidal only
2677	  because the open immersion makes the comparison ring map a (local) isomorphism;
2678	  here the \emph{left} adjoint \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\) is strong monoidal for
2679	  every ring map, so the hypothesis of an isomorphism is dropped entirely.
2680	\end{proof}
2681	
2682	\begin{lemma}
2683	[Pullback preserves the structure sheaf]
2684	  \label{lem:pullback_unit_iso}
2685	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso}
2686	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2687	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes. The pullback of the structure sheaf
2688	  is the structure sheaf: there is a canonical isomorphism
2689	  \[
2690	    f^*\bigl(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\bigr)
2691	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2692	    \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,Y.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf},
2693	    \qquad\text{i.e.}\qquad
2694	    f^* \mathcal{O}_X \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_Y,
2695	  \]
2696	  where \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf} = \mathcal{O}_X\) is
2697	  the monoidal unit of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\).
2698	\end{lemma}
2699	
2700	\begin{proof}
2701	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2702	  The monoidal unit is preserved by the strong-monoidal pullback of
2703	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso}. At the section level the relevant comparison is the
2704	  unit comparison \(\varepsilon\) of the strong-monoidal extension of scalars
2705	  \((\mathtt{extendScalars}\,\psi).\mathtt{Monoidal}\): for a ring map
2706	  \(\psi : R \to S\) it is the canonical isomorphism \(R \otimes_R S \cong S\)
2707	  exhibiting that extension of scalars carries the base ring \(R\) (the
2708	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) unit over \(R\)) to \(S\) (the unit over \(S\)). Assembling
2709	  these sectionwise unit comparisons across the presheaf and sheafifying descends to
2710	  the asserted isomorphism \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\).
2711	
2712	  A morphism \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit} : f^*\mathcal{O}_X \to
2713	  \mathcal{O}_Y\) already exists in Mathlib (it is consumed in
2714	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial.pullback}\), where it is shown to be an isomorphism for
2715	  an open immersion by a finality argument on the comma category of the open
2716	  cover). For a \emph{general} morphism \(f\) that finality argument is unavailable,
2717	  so whether \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\) is itself an isomorphism in general
2718	  must be checked separately; if it is not directly available as an iso, the iso is
2719	  obtained instead from the \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\) unit comparison
2720	  \(\varepsilon\) above, which is an isomorphism for every ring map and hence
2721	  descends to one for every \(f\).
2722	\end{proof}
2723	
2724	\begin{lemma}
2725	[Pullback preserves $\otimes$-invertibility for a general scheme morphism]
2726	  \label{lem:isinvertible_pullback}
2727	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.IsInvertible.pullback}
2728	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible, lem:pullback_tensor_iso,
2729	        lem:pullback_unit_iso}
2730	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let
2731	  \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). If \(M\) is \(\otimes\)-invertible
2732	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), then so is its pullback \(f^*M\):
2733	  \[
2734	    \mathtt{IsInvertible}(M)
2735	      \;\Longrightarrow\;

input.new_string:
This is a genuinely different fact from the open-immersion compatibility
\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, and not a corollary of it. That lemma handles
restriction along an open immersion, and it does so by routing through the
\emph{lax right} adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}/\mathtt{restrictScalars}\), which is
strong monoidal there \emph{only} because an open immersion's structure-sheaf ring
map is a local \emph{isomorphism} (\(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)). A general scheme
morphism has no such isomorphism, so the right-adjoint route is unavailable.

The general case is the \emph{left} adjoint, pullback \(f^* = \) inverse image
\(=(\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\). Conceptually pullback is
``extension of scalars'', and over a single ring extension of scalars is indeed
strong monoidal. But the sheaf-of-modules pullback is defined \emph{abstractly} as
a left adjoint: it has \emph{no} sectionwise value formula and \emph{no} stalkwise
value formula, so one cannot literally attach a sectionwise \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\)
tensorator to \((f^*).\mathrm{obj}\). Moreover the doctrinal transfer that Mathlib
ships, \(\mathtt{Adjunction.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\), produces a \emph{lax}
structure on the \emph{right} adjoint from an oplax structure on the left; the
oplax comparison maps on the left adjoint itself are \emph{not} provided for free.
Consequently the monoidality of \(f^*\) is established here by \emph{local-chart
finality}, not by a global sectionwise tensorator: one exhibits the comparison maps
\(f^*(M \otimes N) \to f^*M \otimes f^*N\) and \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X \to \mathcal{O}_Y\)
and proves each is an isomorphism by restricting to affine charts on which the
comparison functor is \(\mathtt{Final}\) --- precisely the charts where the pullback
\emph{is} locally extension of scalars --- and then globalizing ``iso on every chart
\(\Rightarrow\) global iso''. This is the whole reason the general lemma is a separate
result, with its own \emph{two}-phase proof (the cheap unit comparison and the
genuinely new tensor comparison).

If the general tensor comparison should prove intractable to formalize, a
\emph{flat-restricted} version of \cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} suffices for the
relative Picard consumer: the structure maps it actually pulls back along --- the
projection \(\pi_T\) and the base-change morphisms of \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}
--- are all flat, and over a flat ring map the pullback is exact and the tensorator
argument simplifies. This is recorded as a fallback, not the primary route.

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
  The proof mirrors the structure of \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} but routes
  through the \emph{left} adjoint, where no isomorphism hypothesis on the ring map
  is needed.

  \emph{Step 1 (reduce to the presheaf level).} By
  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}, the substrate tensor \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
  sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
  \(M.\mathrm{val} \otimes^{p} N.\mathrm{val}\), where
  \(\otimes^{p} = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\). The pullback
  commutes with sheafification: the genuine Mathlib lemma
  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\) --- which is general
  (it holds for any morphism of ring-sheaves, and is already consumed in the
  open-immersion case \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) --- supplies a natural
  isomorphism moving \(f^*\) inside the sheafification. The claim therefore reduces
  to the presheaf-level comparison
  \[
    (\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,(A \otimes^{p} B)
      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
    \bigl((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A\bigr)
      \;\otimes^{p}\;
    \bigl((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B\bigr),
  \]
  where \(\varphi\) is the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of rings
  induced by \(f\), and \(A = M.\mathrm{val}\), \(B = N.\mathrm{val}\).

  \emph{Step 2 (the presheaf pullback is strong monoidal).} The presheaf-level
  pullback \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\) is, sectionwise,
  \emph{extension of scalars} along the section ring maps of \(\varphi\) --- the
  left adjoint of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\). Mathlib equips extension of scalars
  along \emph{any} ring homomorphism \(\psi\) with a strong-monoidal structure,
  \((\mathtt{extendScalars}\,\psi).\mathtt{Monoidal}\)
  (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Adjunction.lean}); its
  tensorator is the canonical isomorphism
  \((M \otimes_R N) \otimes_R S \cong (M \otimes_R S) \otimes_S (N \otimes_R S)\),
  valid with \emph{no} hypothesis on \(\psi\). Assembling these sectionwise
  tensorators against the presheaf restriction morphisms furnishes a strong-monoidal
  structure on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), whose monoidal
  comparison \(\mu\) is precisely the presheaf-level isomorphism required by Step~1.
  Sheafifying \(\mu\) and transporting along the
  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) isomorphism of Step~1 yields the asserted
  isomorphism \(f^*(M \otimes_X N) \cong (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N)\). Naturality in
  \(M, N\) is inherited from the naturality of \(\mu\) and of sheafification.

  The contrast with \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is exactly the adjoint used:
  there the comparison is produced from the \emph{right} adjoint
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}/\mathtt{restrictScalars}\), which is strong monoidal only
  because the open immersion makes the comparison ring map a (local) isomorphism;
  here the \emph{left} adjoint \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\) is strong monoidal for
  every ring map, so the hypothesis of an isomorphism is dropped entirely.
\end{proof}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:pullback_unit_iso}
  Because the abstract left-adjoint pullback has no sectionwise value, the
  comparison map must first be \emph{constructed} and then shown to be an isomorphism
  by reduction to local charts. The argument has three parts: (a) build the
  comparison, (b) prove it is an isomorphism by a finality chart-chase, (c) package
  the result as a strong-monoidal structure.

  \emph{(a) The tensor comparison map.} In parallel to the canonical unit comparison
  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\) of \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso},
  one constructs a tensor comparison
  \[
    \mathtt{pullbackObjTensorToTensor} :
      f^*(M \otimes_X N)
      \;\longrightarrow\;
      (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N),
  \]
  the oplax monoidal comparison \(\delta\) of \(f^*\) on the pair \((M, N)\). This map
  is not available off the shelf --- the Mathlib pullback ships only the composition
  coherences \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\), \(\mathtt{pullbackId}\),
  \(\mathtt{pullback\_assoc}\), and \emph{no} tensorator. It is produced from the
  monoidal structure of the \emph{presheaf}-level pullback
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\) (where, unlike the sheaf level, the
  ambient \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}\) on presheaves of modules over a
  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued ring presheaf \emph{does} exist), moved across the
  sheafification using the genuine Mathlib lemma
  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\) --- general (any morphism of
  ring-sheaves) and already consumed in \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} --- which
  commutes \(f^*\) with sheafification. The reconciliation of the right-hand side
  after this move is supplied by the already-established sheafification-monoidality
  brick (``sheafification is monoidal up to the unit'', cf.
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), which the eventual comparison consumes.

  \emph{(b) It is an isomorphism, by local-chart finality.} This is the same
  chart-chase that proves the unit comparison in \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}, run one
  level up. Cover \(Y\) by affine charts; on such a chart \(V \subseteq Y\) factor the
  inclusion as \(V.\iota \mathbin{;} f = g \mathbin{;} U.\iota\) through
  \(g = f.\mathtt{resLE}\,U\,V\), whose comparison functor
  \(\mathtt{Opens.map}\,g.\mathtt{base}\) is \(\mathtt{Final}\) (via
  \(\mathtt{final\_of\_representablyFlat}\)). On a \(\mathtt{Final}\) chart the
  pullback \emph{is} locally extension of scalars, which is \emph{strong monoidal} in
  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\): the local comparison is exactly the \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\)
  tensorator \((M \otimes_R N) \otimes_R S \cong (M \otimes_R S) \otimes_S
  (N \otimes_R S)\), an isomorphism for every ring map with no hypothesis. The
  restricted global \(\mathtt{pullbackObjTensorToTensor}\) is identified with this
  local comparison through the naturality of the
  \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)/\(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\)/\(\mathtt{pullbackCongr}\)
  cluster --- the same glue wired in \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial.pullback}\). Finally
  ``iso on every chart \(\Rightarrow\) global iso'' is the project's
  \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_isIso\_restrict}\) (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}'s
  globalizer). The residual work is the naturality lemma tying the restricted global
  comparison to the local one.

  \emph{(c) Packaging as strong monoidality.} The two comparison isomorphisms --- the
  tensor comparison \(\delta\) just shown and the unit comparison \(\eta = \varepsilon\)
  of \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso} --- are exactly the inputs of the Mathlib wrapper
  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreMonoidal.ofOplaxMonoidal}\), which from an
  \(\mathtt{OplaxMonoidal}\) structure together with \([\mathtt{IsIso}\,\eta]\) and
  \([\forall\,X\,Y,\ \mathtt{IsIso}\,(\delta\,X\,Y)]\) manufactures a
  \(\mathtt{Monoidal}\) (strong) structure on \(f^*\), with \(\mu\mathtt{Iso}\) and
  \(\varepsilon\mathtt{Iso}\) delivering the comparison isomorphisms. Note that the
  underlying \(\mathtt{OplaxMonoidal}\) structure on \(f^*\) is itself \emph{not}
  free (there is no \(\mathtt{leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal}\) transfer; Mathlib ships only
  \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) for the opposite direction), so it is built by
  hand --- as the mate of the lax structure on \(\mathtt{pushforward}\), or directly
  from the presheaf-level extension-of-scalars description on charts --- before
  feeding the two \(\mathtt{IsIso}\) facts to the wrapper. The resulting \(\mu\mathtt{Iso}\)
  is the asserted isomorphism \(f^*(M \otimes_X N) \cong (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N)\),
  natural in \(M\) and \(N\).
\end{proof}
